summary: Goshen Township held a special meeting highlighted by the ceremonial swearing-in of three new police officers: Chris McMillan, Mike Varon, and Laura Idem. The board also approved the 2022 health care, dental, and HSA plans for employees, conducted the 2021 revenue true-up showing an overall increase of $183,260.12, reviewed the 2022 budget, approved 2022 wages for elected officials and non-union employees, renewed the township administrator's employment agreement, and passed nuisance/junk vehicle abatement resolutions.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for October 27 through November 30, 2021
Approved 2022 health care plan with Medical Mutual of Ohio effective 1-1-2022
Approved 2022 dental plan with Superior Dental Care effective 1-1-2022
Approved change to HSA for township employees with contributions of $500 single, $1,000 married/single with children, $1,500 family
Approved 2021 township revenue true-up with overall increase of $183,260.12
Approved township administrator employment agreement for 2022
Approved 2022 wages for elected officials and non-union employees
Approved Resolution R-008-2021 for nuisance abatement at 6972 OH-132
Approved resolution for junk motor vehicle removal at 2305 State Route 28
action_items: Administrator Figueroa to present 2022 budget for formal approval at December 14 meeting
True-up of 2022 budget to occur in February once 2021 is fully closed
Harry Holbert (zoning director) to obtain contractor bid for nuisance abatement on three properties before the 15th
Chief Myers to work on strategic plan for fire/EMS department and make recommendation on deputy chief position
Administrator to monitor ARP/ARPA legislation for expanded spending eligibility and present projects if law changes
Police department to hire one additional officer
key_amounts: $200,000 Community Development Block Grant for Charles Snyder turn lane/road widening project (100% grant funded)
Over $400,000 additional grant for road paving received
Anthem initial quote was 40%+ increase; settled on Medical Mutual at 8.7% increase for 2022 health care
$183,260.12 overall increase in 2021 township revenue true-up
2022 budget: $3.2 million starting unencumbered, $9.2 million revenue, $8.1 million operating, $2.2 million capital
$854,611.52 ARP funds already in bank; additional $854,611 expected in 2022/2023
HSA contributions: $500 single, $1,000 married/single with children, $1,500 family
3% raise recommended for full-time non-union employees; $0.50 to $1.00/hour raise for part-time employees
notable_topics: Swearing-in ceremony for three new police officers (Chris McMillan, Mike Varon, Laura Idem)
2022 health care, dental, and HSA plan approvals
2021 revenue true-up across all township funds
2022 wages for elected officials and non-union employees
Nuisance abatement and junk vehicle removal resolutions
Clean audit for 2019-2020 with no findings
Ohio State Valor Award for Travis and Todd (fire rescue)
Community forum on traffic safety at SR 28/SR 132 Kroger intersection
Charles Snyder road widening project completion and additional paving grant
American Rescue Plan (ARP) fund usage and pending federal legislation
notes: The transcript contains significant repetitive corrupted text near the end (repeated '0 5 0' and 'Motion has been made and seconded' lines), likely a transcription error. The meeting included a 15-minute break at approximately 7:27 PM for refreshments provided by the Citizens Police Academy Alumni (CPAA) to celebrate the new officers. Officer Laura Idem is from Luxembourg and came to the US as an exchange student. Part-time employees had not received a raise in two years. The township is awaiting federal legislation changes to expand eligible uses for ARP funds, potentially for a service garage.
title: BOT Mtg 11.30.21.mp4
